  "findings": [
    "Miswiring of line/load terminals on GFCI/AFCI devices, failing to protect downstream receptacles as required by code",
    "Failure to verify downstream protection using a dedicated GFCI/AFCI tester during active troubleshooting"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Trace and identify line and load conductors at the primary device location",
    "Correctly wire the downstream receptacles to the load side of the GFCI/AFCI device",
    "Perform operational trip tests at all downstream outlets to verify active protection"
  ],
  "rationale": "During active troubleshooting of GFCI/AFCI devices, failing to distinguish between line and load wiring or using shortcuts that bypass downstream protection violates NEC safety mandates. Downstream receptacles must be properly protected and verified rather than wired as single-location only if the design or code requires downstream protection.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 210.8",
    "NEC 210.12"
  ]
}
